zh.lifeSummary: Conn Mor O'Neil, King of Tir Eogain是Henry OR Enrí O'Néill King of Ulster和Gormley Kavanaugh的兒子，於大約1461年出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 泰龍。他於1479年與Lady Eleanor FitzGerald在愛爾蘭結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1493年1月8日在愛爾蘭庫迪 泰龍登加農享年33歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Conn Mor O'Neil, King of Tir Eogain是Henry OR Enrí O'Néill King of Ulster和Gormley Kavanaugh的儿子，于大约1461年出生在爱尔兰庫迪 泰龍。他于1479年与Lady Eleanor FitzGerald在爱尔兰结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他去世于1493年1月8日在爱尔兰庫迪 泰龍登加農享年33岁。

nameMeanings.name: O'Neil,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Conn, Donal, Liam, Siobhan, Aileen, Eamon, Michael Patrick, Seamus, Sean Patrick, William Sean.
Irish (Mayo, Tipperary): variant of   O'Neill  .

variants.name: O'Neill
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Seamus, Siobhan, Dermot, Liam, Nuala, Bridie, Brigid, Conor, Fintan, John Patrick.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Néill ‘descendant of Niall’, an Irish personal name possibly derived from néal ‘cloud’ (see Neil ).

History: Bearers of this surname mostly claim descent from Niall Noígíallach (Niall of the Nine Hostages), a legendary king of Ireland who flourished in the 5th century. His descendants, the Ui Neill dynasty, dominated Ireland, on and off, from the 6th to the 10th century. The surname itself can be traced with some confidence to one of these descendants, namely Niall Glúndubh mac Aodha (died 919), High King of Ireland from 911 to 919, with a power base in Tyrone, Ulster.

nameMeanings.name: King
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English king ‘king’ (Old English cyning, cyng), perhaps acquired by someone with kingly qualities or as a pageant name by someone who had acted the part of a king or had been chosen as the master of ceremonies or ‘king’ of an event such as a tournament, festival or folk ritual. In North America, the surname King has absorbed several European cognates and equivalents with the same meaning, for example German König (see Koenig ) and Küng, French Roy , Slovenian, Croatian, or Serbian Kralj , Polish Krol . It is also very common among African Americans. It is also found as an artificial Jewish surname.
English: occasionally from the Middle English personal name King, originally an Old English nickname from the vocabulary word cyning, cyng ‘king’.
Irish: adopted for a variety of names containing the syllable rí (which means ‘king’ in Irish).

variants.name: Kingdon
meanings: English (southwestern, especially Devon): habitational name from Higher Kingdon in Alverdiscott, or from Kendon in North Bovey, both in Devon. Both are named in Old English from cyning ‘king’ (see King ) or cyne- ‘royal’ + dūn ‘hill’.

variants.name: Chief
meanings: Native American: translation into English (and shortening) of a personal name based on a word, such as Cheyenne veho, meaning ‘chief’. See also King 4.

variants.name: Koenig
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Hans, Erwin, Dieter, Frieda, Monika, Reinhard, Egon, Fritz, Heinz, Wilhelm.

German (mainly König) and Jewish (Ashkenazic; also König): from Middle High German künic, German König ‘king’, hence a German nickname for a servant or retainer of a king (for example, a farmer on a royal demesne), or alternatively a status name for the head of a craftmen's guild, or a society of sharpshooters or minstrels. As a Jewish surname, it is artificial, one of several such Ashkenazic names based on European titles of nobility or royalty. The surname Koenig is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and Poland. In eastern Germany the surname König is in part a Germanized form (translation into German) of Sorbian Kral ‘king’. Similarly, in Czechia, Poland, Slovenia, and Croatia, it was often applied as a translation into German of Czech, Polish, Slovenian, and Croatian cognates Král (see Kral ), Król (see Krol ), and Kralj , respectively. Compare Konig .

variants.name: Kings
meanings: English (Worcestershire): variant of King , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. — Note: The population figure published by the US Census Bureau for this surname in the year 2000 is as high as 434, while the figure for 2010 is supposedly lower than 100.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Tir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Delfin, Alberto, Arsenio, Catalina, Jose, Juanita, Julio, Manuel, Mario, Ninfa, Nydia, Pepe.
Spanish (Tío) and Portuguese: nickname meaning ‘uncle’.
Catalan (Tió): nickname from tió ‘log, stump’ (for burning).

variants.name: Teo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Boon, Chiew, Chin, Eng, Hai, Liang, Ming, Peng, Seng, Sing, Siong, Soo, Ting, Tua.

Chinese: Teochew, Hokkien, and Taiwanese form of the surname 張, see   Zhang   1. The dialects in which this pronunciation is found are spoken in eastern Guangdong, Fujian, and Taiwan, from where some people migrated to Singapore, Malaysia, Thailand, and other parts of Southeast Asia.

Chinese: Teochew, Hokkien, and Taiwanese form of the surname 趙, see   Zhao   1. The dialects in which this pronunciation is found are spoken in eastern Guangdong, Fujian, and Taiwan, from where some people migrated to Singapore, Malaysia, Thailand, and other parts of Southeast Asia.

nameMeanings.name: Eogain
meanings: Irish: From eoghunn, youth. According to the Annals of Ireland it has been Anglicised into Owen and Eugene; in Latin, Eugenius.

nameMeanings.name: Conn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: name meaning ‘chief’, also used as a short form of Connor and of various non-Irish names beginning with the syllable Con- .

nameMeanings.name: Mor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional name from mór ‘large, great’. This was the commonest of all girls’ names in late medieval Ireland, and has continued in use in both Ireland and Scotland to the present day.

en.lifeSummary: Conn Mor O'Neil, King of Tir Eogain was born about 1461, in County Tyrone, Ireland as the son of Henry OR Enrí O'Néill King of Ulster and Gormley Kavanaugh. He married Lady Eleanor FitzGerald in 1479, in Ireland. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 5 daughters. He died on 8 January 1493, in Dungannon, County Tyrone, Ireland, at the age of 33.

fr.lifeSummary: Conn Mor O'Neil, King of Tir Eogain est né environ 1461, à County Tyrone, Irlande, fils de Henry OR Enrí O'Néill King of Ulster et Gormley Kavanaugh. Il a épousé Lady Eleanor FitzGerald en 1479, à Irlande. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé le 8 janvier 1493, à Dungannon, County Tyrone, Irlande, à l’âge de 33 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Conn Mor O'Neil, King of Tir Eogain wurde um 1461 in County Tyrone, Irland, als Sohn von Henry OR Enrí O'Néill King of Ulster und Gormley Kavanaugh geboren. Er hat 1479 in Irland Lady Eleanor FitzGerald geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Er starb am 8. Januar 1493 im Alter von 33 Jahren in Dungannon, County Tyrone, Irland.

it.lifeSummary: Conn Mor O'Neil, King of Tir Eogain è nato il circa 1461 nel County Tyrone, Irlanda come figlio di Henry OR Enrí O'Néill King of Ulster e Gormley Kavanaugh. Lui sposato Lady Eleanor FitzGerald nel 1479, nel Irlanda. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 8 gennaio 1493, nel Dungannon, County Tyrone, Irlanda, all'età di 33 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Conn Mor O'Neil, King of Tir Eogainは約1461年にアイルランドコウントイ トイロンでHenry OR Enrí O'Néill King of UlsterとGormley Kavanaughの息子として生まれた。1479年にアイルランドでLady Eleanor FitzGeraldと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1493年1月8日にアイルランドコウントイ トイロンダンガノンで33歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Conn Mor O'Neil, King of Tir Eogain(은)는 대략 1461년 아일랜드 카운티 타이론에서 Henry OR Enrí O'Néill King of Ulster(와)과 Gormley Kavanaugh의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1479년 아일랜드에서 Lady Eleanor FitzGerald(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1493년 1월 8일 아일랜드 카운티 타이론 던건넌에서 33세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Conn Mor O'Neil, King of Tir Eogain nasceu aproximadamente 1461, em County Tyrone, Irlanda filho de Henry OR Enrí O'Néill King of Ulster e Gormley Kavanaugh. Ele casou-se com Lady Eleanor FitzGerald em 1479, em Irlanda. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele faleceu em 8 de janeiro de 1493, em Dungannon, County Tyrone, Irlanda, com 33 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Conn Mor O'Neil, King of Tir Eogain родился около 1461 г. в местности коунти тироун, Ирландия. Его родителей звали Henry OR Enrí O'Néill King of Ulster и Gormley Kavanaugh. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Eleanor FitzGerald в 1479 г. в местности Ирландия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Он умер 8 января 1493 г. в местности дунганнон, коунти тироун, Ирландия в возрасте 33 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Conn Mor O'Neil, King of Tir Eogain nació aproximadamente 1461, en County Tyrone, Irlanda como hijo de Henry OR Enrí O'Néill King of Ulster y Gormley Kavanaugh. Se casó con Lady Eleanor FitzGerald en 1479, en Irlanda. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 8 de enero de 1493, en Dungannon, County Tyrone, Irlanda, a la edad de 33 años.
